channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The Latest: Netanyahu and Trump to meet at the White House
item.description: As President Trump prepares to meet with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, Trump’s Middle East envoy said that a three to five-year timeline for reconstruction of Gaza is not a viable post-war plan for the battle-torn territory.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Oklahoma Gov. Stitt announces DOGE-OK
item.description: According to the Governor's Office, DOGE-OK will build upon past efficiency initiatives and focus on eliminating wasteful government spending.
item.dc:creator: Terré Gables/KFOR, NewsNation

item.title: US has frozen funding for the UN-backed mission to quell gangs in Haiti, UN says
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The U.S. has notified the United Nations that it is freezing funding to a U.N.-backed mission in Haiti tasked with fighting gangs trying to seize full control of the country’s capital, the U.N. said Tuesday. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER and DÁNICA COTO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Remains of all 67 victims of the deadly plane and chopper collision near DC have been recovered
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — The remains of all 67 victims of last week's midair collision of an American Airlines flight and an Army helicopter near Washington, D.C., have been recovered, authorities said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: SERKAN GURBUZ and MARYCLAIRE DALE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he's exploring option to send jailed US criminals to other countries
item.description: GUATEMALA CITY (AP) — President Donald Trump said Tuesday that he is exploring whether he can send American criminals jailed in the “most severe cases” to be incarcerated in other countries, a day after El Salvador offered to take them in along with migrants in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard, Trump's pick to oversee US spy agencies, clears Senate committee
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Tulsi Gabbard's nomination to be President Donald Trump's director of national intelligence cleared a key Senate committee Tuesday despite concerns raised about her past comments sympathetic to Russia and a meeting with Syria’s now-deposed leader.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: FBI agents sue over Justice Dept. effort to ID employees involved in Trump-related investigations
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — FBI agents who participated in investigations related to President Donald Trump have sued over Justice Department efforts to develop a list of employees involved in those inquiries that they fear could be a precursor to mass firings.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Detroit arsonist caught on video believed to be fleeing to Tennessee or Texas
item.description: Authorities are searching for a suspect involved in a car explosion captured on video in Detroit with the suspect believed to have fled Detroit and may be traveling to either Tennessee or Texas.
item.dc:creator: Holly Galvan Posey, KPRC 2

item.title: What did US get from deals to pause tariffs on Canada and Mexico? Not that much, observers say
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s threatened tariffs against Canada and Mexico — now on hold for a month — risked blowing up North America’s economy.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, MARÍA VERZA and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trayon White expelled from the DC Council for corruption but he's free to run for his old seat
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The D.C. Council voted unanimously Tuesday to expel Councilmember Trayon White, who faces a federal trial on charges of taking tens of thousands of dollars in bribes to influence city contract decisions. White, 40, was arrested by the FBI last August.
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump oil tariffs expected to raise price at the pump
item.description: “Refineries in the Midwest, Great Lakes and Rockies rely more on the Canadian crude oil for sourcing oil for their refineries,” analyst Patrick De Haan said.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: RFK Jr. appears on track to become US health secretary as he wins key Republican senator's support
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Robert F. Kennedy Jr., a vocal vaccine skeptic and activist lawyer, appeared on track to become the nation's health secretary after winning the crucial support of Republican Sen.
item.dc:creator: AMANDA SEITZ and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Agents sue FBI, DOJ amid fears of retaliation over work on Jan. 6 cases
item.description: The FBI and the Department of Justice (DOJ) are being sued by 16 current agents who say their review of those who worked on the cases of some 1,500 Jan. 6 defendants is a preparation to retaliate against agents.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: McDonald's brings back iconic character to kick off Shamrock Shake season
item.description: McDonald's is bringing back another McDonaldland character for the first time in decades to kick off the return of Shamrock Shake season. The company announced Tuesday that Grimace will reunite with his Irish uncle, Uncle O’Grimacey, after stumbling upon his family’s original recipe for the shake.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Tulsa PD issues Kasey Alert for missing woman
item.description: The Oklahoma Highway Patrol has issued a Kasey Alert on behalf of the Tulsa Police Department for 32-year-old Kristie Holmes.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Sellers/KFOR, NewsNation

item.title: At least 20K have accepted Trump's government buyout offer
item.description: At least 20,000 federal employees have accepted what amounts to a buyout from the Trump administration, a source confirmed to The Hill on Tuesday. Employees still have until Feb.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, WGN 9

item.title: Church vandalized by Proud Boys now controls group's trademark, judge rules
item.description: The Proud Boys is barred from selling merchandise using its name or symbols without permission from a Black church once vandalized by the right-wing extremist group's members, after a Washington, D.C., judge on Monday revoked its control over the trademark.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Major League Baseball fires ump amid gambling probe
item.description: Umpire Pat Hoberg was fired after it was revealed he shared betting accounts with a friend who bet on baseball.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Purges at FBI, DOJ trigger 'battle' for career staff
item.description: The Trump administration has removed dozens of Department of Justice (DOJ) and FBI officials and is eyeing firing perhaps thousands more in an unprecedented purge that may just be getting started.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, ABC 4

item.title: 5 things to know about Trump's plan to create a sovereign wealth fund
item.description: President Donald Trump is taking steps to overhaul the way the U.S. government invests its money and change the nature of America’s financial presence abroad.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Wisconsin man, dog found stabbed to death at home hours after police responded to possible break-in
item.description: A Kenosha man and his dog were found dead at a home hours after the Kenosha Police Department investigated a possible break-in there.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China hits US with retaliatory tariffs
item.description: China hit the U.S. back with retaliatory tariffs across the board over President Trump’s enforcement of tariffs on Chinese goods that went into effect on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: Gerber recalls all batches of Soothe N Chew Teething Sticks nationwide
item.description: Gerber Products Company has announced a nationwide recall and discontinuation of all batches of its Soothe N Chew Teething Sticks due to a potential choking hazard for infants and young children.
item.dc:creator: Reagan Wynn, Fox 5

item.title: USAID leaders escorted out of building after blocking DOGE access to secure systems
item.description: Officials said USAID members were eventually unsuccessful and DOGE teams were able to gain access to some of the agency’s classified information, including intelligence reports.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KRON4

item.title: China to file lawsuit with WTO, vows 'countermeasures' in response to Trump tariffs
item.description: In a statement, the ministry’s spokesperson vowed further “countermeasures” against the United States but did not specify what steps it would take.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Canadian fans boo U.S. National Anthem at hockey game amid trade war
item.description: Canadians aren’t too happy with the tariffs being imposed on their country by U.S. President Donald Trump. Trump signed an order Saturday to impose stiff tariffs on imports from Canada as well as Mexico and China, which drew swift retaliation and sparked a trade war.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump puts tariffs on Canada, Mexico and China, spurring trade war as North American allies respond
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Saturday signed an order to impose stiff tariffs on imports from Mexico, Canada and China, drawing swift retaliation and an undeniable sense of betrayal from the country's North American neighbors as a trade war erupted among the longtime allies.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, ZEKE MILLER, ROB GILLIES and CHRISTOPHER SHERMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Groundhog Day 2025: Punxsutawney Phil sees his shadow
item.description: PUNXSUTAWNEY, Pa. (WTAJ) — Each year Punxsutawney Phil predicts whether there will be six more weeks of winter or early spring when he emerges from his home in Gobbler's Knob on Groundhog Day. It's official! Punxsutawney Phil saw his shadow, meaning a prediction of six more weeks of winter.
item.dc:creator: Bill Shannon, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Why February's Supplemental Security Income checks are coming early
item.description: If you receive Supplemental Security Income from the Social Security Administration, you know the last two payments have been a bit wonky.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KTLA 5

item.title: Michigan priest has license revoked after imitating Musk's gesture at Pro-Life Summit
item.description: The priest, Calvin Robinson, attended the National Pro-Life Summit in Washington, D.C. last month. Toward the end of his remarks at the Jan. 25 event, he mimicked Musk’s salute, which made the rounds online.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Army releases name of 3rd soldier who died in helicopter and airliner crash
item.description: The Army on Saturday released the name of the third soldier who died Wednesday when an Army helicopter collided with an American Airlines jet near Reagan National Airport in D.C.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: CBS agrees to hand over '60 Minutes' Harris interview transcripts to FCC
item.description: CBS says it will turn over an unedited transcript of its October interview with Kamala Harris to the Federal Communications Commission, part of President Donald Trump's ongoing fight with the network over how it handled a story about his opponent.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Data from the deadliest U.S. air accident in a generation show conflicting altitude readings
item.description: Recovery crews and divers searched the Potomac River for remains and cleared wreckage Saturday from the midair collision of a passenger jet and Army helicopter that killed 67 people.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says he ordered precision military strike against senior ISIS planner
item.description: President Trump said he ordered precision air strikes on Saturday morning targeting a "senior ISIS Attack planner" and others the unnamed individual recruited and led in Somalia.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, ABC 4

item.title: A medical plane carrying a child patient and 5 others crashes in Philadelphia, setting homes ablaze
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — A medical transport jet with a child patient, her mother and four others aboard crashed into a Philadelphia neighborhood shortly after takeoff Friday evening, exploding in a fireball that engulfed several homes.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and MATT ROURKE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Man agrees to plead guilty for flying drone that damaged firefighting aircraft in LA wildfire
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The pilot of a drone that crashed into a firefighting plane, leaving a gaping hole and grounding the aircraft during the deadly Palisades Fire in Los Angeles, has agreed to plead guilty to a misdemeanor count of unsafely operating a drone, federal authorities said Friday.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING and OLGA R. RODRIGUEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Tour guide at former Colorado gold mine fell out of elevator after apparently not latching door
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A tour guide killed at a former Colorado gold mine last year fell out of a crowded elevator taking tourists below ground after apparently not latching its door closed, documents obtained by The Associated Press show.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, WGN 9
